Details: Biological degradation of 1,4-dioxane was reported to be effective in laboratory studies (up to 94 percent removal). The best-characterized aerobic bacteria strain is a monooxygenases-expressing strain, Pseudonocardia dioxanivorans CB1190. CB1190 was reported to effectively degrade 1,4-dioxane by more than 80 percent in several days at room temperature [2079, 2039]. Another species, Flavobacterium, was found to co-metabolize 1,4-dioxane with presence of tetrahydrofuran [2107]. Flavobacterium degraded 1,4-dioxane up to 94 percent in two to three weeks at room temperature in the presence of tetrahydrofuran. Biological treatment may not be feasible for full-scale water treatment due to a number of considerations including reliability, robustness, and sustainability issues.
